mechanism_narrative: Unexpectedly strong retail sales growth signals rising consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and equity valuations upward. The effect is monotonic: higher sales translate directly into higher forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail and food services sales at 362.7B in July 2010, above recession trough
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negatively correlated with RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests (Rank IC >= -0.03 for any 2-year window) or the relationship vanishes in cross-validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near zero, the Federal Reserve has exhausted conventional easing, removing the headwind of imminent rate hikes. The Fed's forward guidance and asset purchases become the dominant channel, supporting equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate at 0.18% in July 2010
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS <= 0.5%, the forward 20-day SPX return distribution does not differ significantly from periods with higher funds rates (Kolmogorov-Smirnov test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in employment and unemployment (rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE) signals broad-based labor market healing. This boosts household income expectations, consumer confidence, and spending, driving equity prices higher over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Nonfarm payrolls at 130.2M, unemployment at 9.5% in July 2010
failure_condition: In months where both PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases, 20-day forward returns are not reliably positive (positive return frequency < 60%) or the interaction term is insignificant in regression.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is rising from a cyclical trough, it indicates expanding real activity and corporate investment. This revives earnings expectations and reflects a self-reinforcing recovery cycle. The signal is regime-specific: it only works in the early expansion phase.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.2% for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production index at 92.54 in June 2010 after bottoming in 2009
regime_hint: early expansion phase after recession trough
failure_condition: Fails if 60-day forward returns do not exceed a 5% gain threshold in at least 70% of qualifying occasions, or if the regime classifier misidentifies the early expansion phase.

mechanism_narrative: A very high unemployment rate (above 8%) signals persistent labor market slack, depressed wage growth, and weak consumer spending. This environment weighs on corporate revenues and investor sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the near term.
failure_condition: When UNRATE >= 8%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is positive (contradicting negative direction) or the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Low interest rates combined with strong retail sales create a powerful short-term impulse: cheap credit amplifies consumer spending, which immediately boosts earnings in the retail and leisure sectors. The interaction is synergistic and should produce rapid equity gains.
failure_condition: In 5-day windows following joint activation, the SPX return is not positive more than 65% of the time or the mean return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading indicator of residential investment. In the recovery phase, rising starts signal improving household wealth, construction employment, and durable goods demand. This drives a sustained lift in economic activity and equities over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month increase > 5% compared to the previous year's same month
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts at 549K in June 2010, up from worst of recession
failure_condition: When HOUST rises more than 5% YoY, the 60-day forward SPX return is less than zero on average, or the signal does not predict returns above the baseline in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) regime, the level of the fed funds rate becomes a stale indicator; instead, the Fed's commitment to low rates sustains risk appetite. Any maintaining of ZIRP (no rate hike) should be interpreted as dovish, supporting equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% for at least 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been at 0.18% since December 2008
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% for 3+ months, the subsequent 20-day SPX returns are not significantly different from periods with rates between 0.25% and 1% (t-test p > 0.10).
